Appbar/Appbar.title: Appbar

dark.description: Whether the background color is a dark color. A dark appbar will render light text and vice-versa.

mode.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Mode of the Appbar.
- `small` - Appbar with default height (64).
- `medium` - Appbar with medium height (112).
- `large` - Appbar with large height (152).
- `center-aligned` - Appbar with default height and center-aligned title.

elevated.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Whether Appbar background should have the elevation along with primary color pigment.

safeAreaInsets.description: Safe area insets for the Appbar. This can be used to avoid elements like the navigation bar on Android and bottom safe area on iOS.

Appbar/AppbarHeader.title: Appbar.Header

dark.description: Whether the background color is a dark color. A dark header will render light text and vice-versa.

statusBarHeight.description:
Extra padding to add at the top of header to account for translucent status bar.
This is automatically handled on iOS >= 11 including iPhone X using `SafeAreaView`.
If you are using Expo, we assume translucent status bar and set a height for status bar automatically.
Pass `0` or a custom value to disable the default behaviour, and customize the height.

mode.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Mode of the Appbar.
- `small` - Appbar with default height (64).
- `medium` - Appbar with medium height (112).
- `large` - Appbar with large height (152).
- `center-aligned` - Appbar with default height and center-aligned title.

elevated.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Whether Appbar background should have the elevation along with primary color pigment.

Banner.title: Banner

children.description: Content that will be displayed inside banner.

icon.description: Icon to display for the `Banner`. Can be an image.

actions.description:
Action items to shown in the banner.
An action item should contain the following properties:

- `label`: label of the action button (required)
- `onPress`: callback that is called when button is pressed (required)

To customize button you can pass other props that button component takes.

contentStyle.description:
Style of banner's inner content.
Use this prop to apply custom width for wide layouts.

elevation.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Changes Banner shadow and background on iOS and Android.

maxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a text font can reach.

onShowAnimationFinished.description: Optional callback that will be called after the opening animation finished running normally

onHideAnimationFinished.description: Optional callback that will be called after the closing animation finished running normally

BottomNavigation/BottomNavigation.title: BottomNavigation

methods.name: SceneMap
methods.docblock:
Function which takes a map of route keys to components.
Pure components are used to minimize re-rendering of the pages.
This drastically improves the animation performance.

type.name: signature

value.name: ReactComponentType

methods.description:
Function which takes a map of route keys to components.
Pure components are used to minimize re-rendering of the pages.
This drastically improves the animation performance.

shifting.description:
Whether the shifting style is used, the active tab icon shifts up to show the label and the inactive tabs won't have a label.

By default, this is `false` with theme version 3 and `true` when you have more than 3 tabs.
Pass `shifting={false}` to explicitly disable this animation, or `shifting={true}` to always use this animation.
Note that you need at least 2 tabs be able to run this animation.

labeled.description: Whether to show labels in tabs. When `false`, only icons will be displayed.

compact.description: Whether tabs should be spread across the entire width.

navigationState.description:
State for the bottom navigation. The state should contain the following properties:

- `index`: a number representing the index of the active route in the `routes` array
- `routes`: an array containing a list of route objects used for rendering the tabs

Each route object should contain the following properties:

- `key`: a unique key to identify the route (required)
- `title`: title of the route to use as the tab label
- `focusedIcon`:  icon to use as the focused tab icon, can be a string, an image source or a react component @renamed Renamed from 'icon' to 'focusedIcon' in v5.x
- `unfocusedIcon`:  icon to use as the unfocused tab icon, can be a string, an image source or a react component @supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
- `color`: color to use as background color for shifting bottom navigation @deprecatedProperty In v5.x works only with theme version 2.
- `badge`: badge to show on the tab icon, can be `true` to show a dot, `string` or `number` to show text.
- `accessibilityLabel`: accessibility label for the tab button
- `testID`: test id for the tab button

Example:

```js
{
  index: 1,
  routes: [
    { key: 'music', title: 'Favorites', focusedIcon: 'heart', unfocusedIcon: 'heart-outline'},
    { key: 'albums', title: 'Albums', focusedIcon: 'album' },
    { key: 'recents', title: 'Recents', focusedIcon: 'history' },
    { key: 'notifications', title: 'Notifications', focusedIcon: 'bell', unfocusedIcon: 'bell-outline' },
  ]
}
```

`BottomNavigation` is a controlled component, which means the `index` needs to be updated via the `onIndexChange` callback.

onIndexChange.description:
Callback which is called on tab change, receives the index of the new tab as argument.
The navigation state needs to be updated when it's called, otherwise the change is dropped.

tsType.name: signature
signature

type.name: signature

renderIcon.description: Callback which returns a React Element to be used as tab icon.

tsType.name: signature

type.name: signature

renderLabel.description: Callback which React Element to be used as tab label.

type.name: intersection

elements.name: signature

renderTouchable.description:
Callback which returns a React element to be used as the touchable for the tab item.
Renders a `TouchableRipple` on Android and `Pressable` on iOS.

getAccessibilityLabel.description:
Get accessibility label for the tab button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the tab.
Uses `route.accessibilityLabel` by default.

tsType.name: signature
tsType.raw: (props: { route: Route }) => boolean | number | string | undefined

getBadge.description: Get badge for the tab, uses `route.badge` by default.

getColor.description: Get color for the tab, uses `route.color` by default.

getLabelText.description: Get label text for the tab, uses `route.title` by default. Use `renderLabel` to replace label component.

getLazy.description: Get lazy for the current screen. Uses true by default.

getTestID.description: Get the id to locate this tab button in tests, uses `route.testID` by default.

onTabPress.description: Function to execute on tab press. It receives the route for the pressed tab, useful for things like scroll to top.

onTabLongPress.description: Function to execute on tab long press. It receives the route for the pressed tab, useful for things like custom action when longed pressed.

activeColor.description: Custom color for icon and label in the active tab.

inactiveColor.description: Custom color for icon and label in the inactive tab.

sceneAnimationEnabled.description:
Whether animation is enabled for scenes transitions in `shifting` mode.
By default, the scenes cross-fade during tab change when `shifting` is enabled.
Specify `sceneAnimationEnabled` as `false` to disable the animation.

sceneAnimationType.description:
The scene animation effect. Specify `'shifting'` for a different effect.
By default, 'opacity' will be used.

keyboardHidesNavigationBar.description:
Whether the bottom navigation bar is hidden when keyboard is shown.
On Android, this works best when [`windowSoftInputMode`](https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/manifest/activity-element#wsoft) is set to `adjustResize`.

tsType.name: signature

safeAreaInsets.description:
Safe area insets for the tab bar. This can be used to avoid elements like the navigation bar on Android and bottom safe area on iOS.
The bottom insets for iOS is added by default. You can override the behavior with this option.

barStyle.description:
Style for the bottom navigation bar.  You can pass a custom background color here:

```js
barStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#694fad' }}
```

labelMaxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a label font can reach.

testID.description: TestID used for testing purposes

State for the bottom navigation. The state should contain the following properties:

- `index`: a number representing the index of the active route in the `routes` array
- `routes`: an array containing a list of route objects used for rendering the tabs

Each route object should contain the following properties:

- `key`: a unique key to identify the route (required)

Button/Button.title: Button

mode.description:
Mode of the button. You can change the mode to adjust the styling to give it desired emphasis.
- `text` - flat button without background or outline, used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options.
- `outlined` - button with an outline without background, typically used for important, but not primary action – represents medium emphasis.
- `contained` - button with a background color, used for important action, have the most visual impact and high emphasis.
- `elevated` - button with a background color and elevation, used when absolutely necessary e.g. button requires visual separation from a patterned background. @supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
- `contained-tonal` - button with a secondary background color, an alternative middle ground between contained and outlined buttons. @supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

dark.description:
Whether the color is a dark color. A dark button will render light text and vice-versa. Only applicable for:
 * `contained` mode for theme version 2
 * `contained`, `contained-tonal` and `elevated` modes for theme version 3.

compact.description: Use a compact look, useful for `text` buttons in a row.

color.description:
@deprecated Deprecated in v5.x - use `buttonColor` or `textColor` instead.
Custom text color for flat button, or background color for contained button.

loading.description: Whether to show a loading indicator.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled. A disabled button is greyed out and `onPress` is not called on touch.

uppercase.description: Make the label text uppercased. Note that this won't work if you pass React elements as children.

background.description:
Type of background drawabale to display the feedback (Android).
https://reactnative.dev/docs/pressable#rippleconfig

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the button.

accessibilityHint.description: Accessibility hint for the button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the button.

accessibilityRole.description: Accessibility role for the button. The "button" role is set by default.

onPressIn.description: Function to execute as soon as the touchable element is pressed and invoked even before onPress.

onPressOut.description: Function to execute as soon as the touch is released even before onPress.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long press.

delayLongPress.description: The number of milliseconds a user must touch the element before executing `onLongPress`.

contentStyle.description:
Style of button's inner content.
Use this prop to apply custom height and width, to set a custom padding or to set the icon on the right with `flexDirection: 'row-reverse'`.

maxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a text font can reach.

hitSlop.description: Sets additional distance outside of element in which a press can be detected.

testID.description: testID to be used on tests.

Card/Card.title: Card
Card/Card.description:
A card is a sheet of material that serves as an entry point to more detailed information.

## Usage
```js
import * as React from 'react';
import { Avatar, Button, Card, Text } from 'react-native-paper';

const LeftContent = props => <Avatar.Icon {...props} icon="folder" />

const MyComponent = () => (
  <Card>
    <Card.Title title="Card Title" subtitle="Card Subtitle" left={LeftContent} />
    <Card.Content>
      <Text variant="titleLarge">Card title</Text>
      <Text variant="bodyMedium">Card content</Text>
    </Card.Content>
    <Card.Cover source={{ uri: 'https://picsum.photos/700' }} />
    <Card.Actions>
      <Button>Cancel</Button>
      <Button>Ok</Button>
    </Card.Actions>
  </Card>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

data.description:
A card is a sheet of material that serves as an entry point to more detailed information.

## Usage
```js
import * as React from 'react';
import { Avatar, Button, Card, Text } from 'react-native-paper';

const LeftContent = props => <Avatar.Icon {...props} icon="folder" />

const MyComponent = () => (
  <Card>
    <Card.Title title="Card Title" subtitle="Card Subtitle" left={LeftContent} />
    <Card.Content>
      <Text variant="titleLarge">Card title</Text>
      <Text variant="bodyMedium">Card content</Text>
    </Card.Content>
    <Card.Cover source={{ uri: 'https://picsum.photos/700' }} />
    <Card.Actions>
      <Button>Cancel</Button>
      <Button>Ok</Button>
    </Card.Actions>
  </Card>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

mode.description:
Mode of the Card.
- `elevated` - Card with elevation.
- `contained` - Card without outline and elevation @supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
- `outlined` - Card with an outline.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long press.

onPressIn.description: Function to execute as soon as the touchable element is pressed and invoked even before onPress.

onPressOut.description: Function to execute as soon as the touch is released even before onPress.

delayLongPress.description: The number of milliseconds a user must touch the element before executing `onLongPress`.

disabled.description: If true, disable all interactions for this component.

elevation.description: Changes Card shadow and background on iOS and Android.

testID.description: Pass down testID from card props to touchable

accessible.description: Pass down accessible from card props to touchable

Card/CardTitle.title: Card.Title

title.description: Text for the title. Note that this will only accept a string or `<Text>`-based node.

titleNumberOfLines.description: Number of lines for the title.

titleVariant.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Title text variant defines appropriate text styles for type role and its size.
Available variants:

 Display: `displayLarge`, `displayMedium`, `displaySmall`

 Headline: `headlineLarge`, `headlineMedium`, `headlineSmall`

 Title: `titleLarge`, `titleMedium`, `titleSmall`

 Label:  `labelLarge`, `labelMedium`, `labelSmall`

 Body: `bodyLarge`, `bodyMedium`, `bodySmall`

subtitle.description: Text for the subtitle. Note that this will only accept a string or `<Text>`-based node.

subtitleNumberOfLines.description: Number of lines for the subtitle.

subtitleVariant.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Subtitle text variant defines appropriate text styles for type role and its size.
Available variants:

 Display: `displayLarge`, `displayMedium`, `displaySmall`

 Headline: `headlineLarge`, `headlineMedium`, `headlineSmall`

 Title: `titleLarge`, `titleMedium`, `titleSmall`

 Label:  `labelLarge`, `labelMedium`, `labelSmall`

 Body: `bodyLarge`, `bodyMedium`, `bodySmall`

left.description: Callback which returns a React element to display on the left side.

leftStyle.description: Style for the left element wrapper.

right.description: Callback which returns a React element to display on the right side.

rightStyle.description: Style for the right element wrapper.

titleMaxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a title font can reach.

subtitleMaxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a subtitle font can reach.

Chip/Chip.title: Chip

mode.description:
Mode of the chip.
- `flat` - flat chip without outline.
- `outlined` - chip with an outline.

icon.description: Icon to display for the `Chip`. Both icon and avatar cannot be specified.

avatar.description: Avatar to display for the `Chip`. Both icon and avatar cannot be specified.

closeIcon.description: Icon to display as the close button for the `Chip`. The icon appears only when the onClose prop is specified.

selectedColor.description:
Whether to style the chip color as selected.
Note: With theme version 3 `selectedColor` doesn't apply to the `icon`.
      If you want specify custom color for the `icon`, render your own `Icon` component.

showSelectedOverlay.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Whether to display overlay on selected chip

showSelectedCheck.description:
Whether to display default check icon on selected chip.
Note: Check will not be shown if `icon` is specified. If specified, `icon` will be shown regardless of `selected`.

disabled.description: Whether the chip is disabled. A disabled chip is greyed out and `onPress` is not called on touch.

background.description:
Type of background drawabale to display the feedback (Android).
https://reactnative.dev/docs/pressable#rippleconfig

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the chip. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the chip.

closeIconAccessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the close icon. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the close icon.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long press.

onPressIn.description: Function to execute as soon as the touchable element is pressed and invoked even before onPress.

onPressOut.description: Function to execute as soon as the touch is released even before onPress.

onClose.description: Function to execute on close button press. The close button appears only when this prop is specified.

delayLongPress.description: The number of milliseconds a user must touch the element before executing `onLongPress`.

compact.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Sets smaller horizontal paddings `12dp` around label, when there is only label.

elevated.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Whether chip should have the elevation.

hitSlop.description: Sets additional distance outside of element in which a press can be detected.

testID.description: Pass down testID from chip props to touchable for Detox tests.

ellipsizeMode.description: Ellipsize Mode for the children text

maxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a text font can reach.

FAB/FAB.title: FAB

icon.description: Icon to display for the `FAB`. It's optional only if `label` is defined.

label.description: Optional label for extended `FAB`. It's optional only if `icon` is defined.

Type of background drawabale to display the feedback (Android).
https://reactnative.dev/docs/pressable#rippleconfig

accessibilityLabel.description:

Uses `label` by default if specified.

accessibilityState.description: Accessibility state for the FAB. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the FAB.

animated.description: Whether an icon change is animated.

small.description:
@deprecated Deprecated in v.5x - use prop size="small".

 Whether FAB is mini-sized, used to create visual continuity with other elements. This has no effect if `label` is specified.

color.description: Custom color for the icon and label of the `FAB`.

disabled.description: Whether `FAB` is disabled. A disabled button is greyed out and `onPress` is not called on touch.

loading.description: Whether to show a loading indicator.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long press.

delayLongPress.description: The number of milliseconds a user must touch the element before executing `onLongPress`.

size.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Size of the `FAB`.
- `small` - FAB with small height (40).
- `medium` - FAB with default medium height (56).
- `large` - FAB with large height (96).

customSize.description: Custom size for the `FAB`. This prop takes precedence over size prop

mode.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Mode of the `FAB`. You can change the mode to adjust the the shadow:
- `flat` - button without a shadow.
- `elevated` - button with a shadow.

variant.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Color mappings variant for combinations of container and icon colors.

testID.description: TestID used for testing purposes

FAB/FABGroup.title: FAB.Group

tsType.name: Array

elements.name: signature

actions.description:
Action items to display in the form of a speed dial.
An action item should contain the following properties:
- `icon`: icon to display (required)
- `label`: optional label text
- `color`: custom icon color of the action item
- `labelTextColor`: custom label text color of the action item
- `accessibilityLabel`: accessibility label for the action, uses label by default if specified
- `accessibilityHint`: accessibility hint for the action
- `style`: pass additional styles for the fab item, for example, `backgroundColor`
- `containerStyle`: pass additional styles for the fab item label container, for example, `backgroundColor` @supported Available in 5.x
- `wrapperStyle`: pass additional styles for the wrapper of the action item.
- `labelStyle`: pass additional styles for the fab item label, for example, `fontSize`
- `labelMaxFontSizeMultiplier`: specifies the largest possible scale a title font can reach.
- `onPress`: callback that is called when `FAB` is pressed (required)
- `onLongPress`: callback that is called when `FAB` is long pressed
- `toggleStackOnLongPress`: callback that is called when `FAB` is long pressed
- `size`: size of action item. Defaults to `small`. @supported Available in v5.x
- `testID`: testID to be used on tests
- `rippleColor`: color of the ripple effect.

icon.description:
Icon to display for the `FAB`.
You can toggle it based on whether the speed dial is open to display a different icon.

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the FAB. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the FAB.

backdropColor.description: Custom backdrop color for opened speed dial background.

onPress.description: Function to execute on pressing the `FAB`.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long pressing the `FAB`.

toggleStackOnLongPress.description: Makes actions stack appear on long press instead of on press.

delayLongPress.description: Changes the delay for long press reaction.

enableLongPressWhenStackOpened.description: Allows for onLongPress when stack is opened.

open.description: Whether the speed dial is open.

onStateChange.description:
Callback which is called on opening and closing the speed dial.
The open state needs to be updated when it's called, otherwise the change is dropped.

style.description:
Style for the group. You can use it to pass additional styles if you need.
For example, you can set an additional padding if you have a tab bar at the bottom.

fabStyle.description: Style for the FAB. It allows to pass the FAB button styles, such as backgroundColor.

variant.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Color mappings variant for combinations of container and icon colors.

testID.description: Pass down testID from Group props to FAB.

padding.description: Whether to apply padding to the helper text.

disabled.description: Whether the text input tied with helper text is disabled.

iconColor.description:

Color of the icon.

containerColor.description: Background color of the icon container.

selected.description:

Whether icon button is selected. A selected button receives alternative combination of icon and container colors.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled. A disabled button is greyed out and `onPress` is not called on touch.

animated.description: Whether an icon change is animated.

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the button.

contentStyle.description:
Style of button's inner content.
Use this prop to apply custom height and width or to set a custom padding`.

testID.description: TestID used for testing purposes

loading.description: Whether to show a loading indicator.

List/ListAccordion.title: List.Accordion

title.description: Title text for the list accordion.

description.description: Description text for the list accordion.

left.description: Callback which returns a React element to display on the left side.

right.description: Callback which returns a React element to display on the right side.

expanded.description:
Whether the accordion is expanded
If this prop is provided, the accordion will behave as a "controlled component".
You'll need to update this prop when you want to toggle the component or on `onPress`.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long press.

delayLongPress.description: The number of milliseconds a user must touch the element before executing `onLongPress`.

background.description:
Type of background drawabale to display the feedback (Android).
https://reactnative.dev/docs/pressable#rippleconfig

style.description: Style that is passed to the root TouchableRipple container.

containerStyle.description: Style that is passed to the outermost container that wraps the entire content, including left and right items and both title and description.

contentStyle.description: Style that is passed to the content container, which wraps the title and description.

titleStyle.description: Style that is passed to Title element.

descriptionStyle.description: Style that is passed to Description element.

titleNumberOfLines.description:
Truncate Title text such that the total number of lines does not
exceed this number.

descriptionNumberOfLines.description:
Truncate Description text such that the total number of lines does not
exceed this number.

titleMaxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a title font can reach.

descriptionMaxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a description font can reach.

id.description: Id is used for distinguishing specific accordion when using List.AccordionGroup. Property is required when using List.AccordionGroup and has no impact on behavior when using standalone List.Accordion.

testID.description: TestID used for testing purposes

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the TouchableRipple. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the touchable.

hitSlop.description:
Amount of space between the touchable area and the edge of the component.
This can be used to enlarge the touchable area beyond the visible component.

List/ListAccordionGroup.title: List.AccordionGroup
List/ListAccordionGroup.description:
List.AccordionGroup allows to control a group of List Accordions. `id` prop for List.Accordion is required in order for group to work.
List.AccordionGroup can be a controlled or uncontrolled component. The example shows the uncontrolled version.
At most one Accordion can be expanded at a given time.

## Usage
```js
import * as React from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import { List } from 'react-native-paper';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <List.AccordionGroup>
    <List.Accordion title="Accordion 1" id="1">
      <List.Item title="Item 1" />
    </List.Accordion>
    <List.Accordion title="Accordion 2" id="2">
      <List.Item title="Item 2" />
    </List.Accordion>
    <View>
      <Text>
        List.Accordion can be wrapped because implementation uses React.Context.
      </Text>
      <List.Accordion title="Accordion 3" id="3">
        <List.Item title="Item 3" />
      </List.Accordion>
    </View>
  </List.AccordionGroup>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

data.description:
List.AccordionGroup allows to control a group of List Accordions. `id` prop for List.Accordion is required in order for group to work.
List.AccordionGroup can be a controlled or uncontrolled component. The example shows the uncontrolled version.
At most one Accordion can be expanded at a given time.

## Usage
```js
import * as React from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import { List } from 'react-native-paper';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <List.AccordionGroup>
    <List.Accordion title="Accordion 1" id="1">
      <List.Item title="Item 1" />
    </List.Accordion>
    <List.Accordion title="Accordion 2" id="2">
      <List.Item title="Item 2" />
    </List.Accordion>
    <View>
      <Text>
        List.Accordion can be wrapped because implementation uses React.Context.
      </Text>
      <List.Accordion title="Accordion 3" id="3">
        <List.Item title="Item 3" />
      </List.Accordion>
    </View>
  </List.AccordionGroup>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

Menu/Menu.title: Menu

visible.description: Whether the Menu is currently visible.

anchor.description: The anchor to open the menu from. In most cases, it will be a button that opens the menu.

anchorPosition.description:
Whether the menu should open at the top of the anchor or at its bottom.
Applied only when anchor is a node, not an x/y position.

statusBarHeight.description:
Extra margin to add at the top of the menu to account for translucent status bar on Android.
If you are using Expo, we assume translucent status bar and set a height for status bar automatically.
Pass `0` or a custom value to and customize it.
This is automatically handled on iOS.

onDismiss.description: Callback called when Menu is dismissed. The `visible` prop needs to be updated when this is called.

overlayAccessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the overlay. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps outside the menu.

elevation.description:
Elevation level of the menu's content. Shadow styles are calculated based on this value. Default `backgroundColor` is taken from the corresponding `theme.colors.elevation` property. By default equals `2`.
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

mode.description:
Mode of the menu's content.
- `elevated` - Surface with a shadow and background color corresponding to set `elevation` value.
- `flat` - Surface without a shadow, with the background color corresponding to set `elevation` value.

@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

testID.description: testID to be used on tests.

ProgressBar.title: ProgressBar

animatedValue.description:
Animated value (between 0 and 1). This tells the progress bar to rely on this value to animate it.
Note: It should not be used in parallel with the `progress` prop.

progress.description:
Progress value (between 0 and 1).
Note: It should not be used in parallel with the `animatedValue` prop.

color.description: Color of the progress bar. The background color will be calculated based on this but you can change it by passing `backgroundColor` to `style` prop.

indeterminate.description: If the progress bar will show indeterminate progress.

visible.description: Whether to show the ProgressBar (true, the default) or hide it (false).

fillStyle.description: Style of filled part of the ProgresBar.

testID.description: testID to be used on tests.

SegmentedButtons/SegmentedButtons.title: SegmentedButtons

tsType.name: Array

elements.name: signature

buttons.description:
Buttons to display as options in toggle button.
Button should contain the following properties:
- `value`: value of button (required)
- `icon`: icon to display for the item
- `disabled`: whether the button is disabled
- `accessibilityLabel`: acccessibility label for the button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the button.
- `checkedColor`: custom color for checked Text and Icon
- `uncheckedColor`: custom color for unchecked Text and Icon
- `onPress`: callback that is called when button is pressed
- `label`: label text of the button
- `showSelectedCheck`: show optional check icon to indicate selected state
- `style`: pass additional styles for the button
- `testID`: testID to be used on tests

density.description: Density is applied to the height, to allow usage in denser UIs

Snackbar.title: Snackbar

visible.description: Whether the Snackbar is currently visible.

action.description:
Label and press callback for the action button. It should contain the following properties:
- `label` - Label of the action button
- `onPress` - Callback that is called when action button is pressed.

icon.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Icon to display when `onIconPress` is defined. Default will be `close` icon.

rippleColor.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Color of the ripple effect.

onIconPress.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Function to execute on icon button press. The icon button appears only when this prop is specified.

iconAccessibilityLabel.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Accessibility label for the icon button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the button.

duration.description: The duration for which the Snackbar is shown.

onDismiss.description: Callback called when Snackbar is dismissed. The `visible` prop needs to be updated when this is called.

elevation.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Changes Snackbar shadow and background on iOS and Android.

maxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a text font can reach.

wrapperStyle.description: Style for the wrapper of the snackbar

contentStyle.description: Style for the content of the snackbar

testID.description: TestID used for testing purposes

Surface.title: Surface

elevation.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Changes shadows and background on iOS and Android.
Used to create UI hierarchy between components.

Note: If `mode` is set to `flat`, Surface doesn't have a shadow.

Note: In version 2 the `elevation` prop was accepted via `style` prop i.e. `style={{ elevation: 4 }}`.
It's no longer supported with theme version 3 and you should use `elevation` property instead.

mode.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3
Mode of the Surface.
- `elevated` - Surface with a shadow and background color corresponding to set `elevation` value.
- `flat` - Surface without a shadow, with the background color corresponding to set `elevation` value.

testID.description: TestID used for testing purposes

Switch/Switch.title: Switch

disabled.description: Disable toggling the switch.

value.description: Value of the switch, true means 'on', false means 'off'.

onValueChange.description: Callback called with the new value when it changes.

TextInput/TextInput.title: TextInput

mode.description:
Mode of the TextInput.
- `flat` - flat input with an underline.
- `outlined` - input with an outline.

In `outlined` mode, the background color of the label is derived from `colors?.background` in theme or the `backgroundColor` style.
This component render TextInputOutlined or TextInputFlat based on that props

left.description: The adornment placed on the left side of the input. It can be either `TextInput.Icon` or `TextInput.Affix`.

right.description: The adornment placed on the right side of the input. It can be either `TextInput.Icon` or `TextInput.Affix`.

disabled.description: If true, user won't be able to interact with the component.

label.description: The text or component to use for the floating label.

error.description: Whether to style the TextInput with error style.

onChangeText.description: Callback that is called when the text input's text changes. Changed text is passed as an argument to the callback handler.

selectionColor.description:
Selection color of the input. On iOS, it sets both the selection color and cursor color.
On Android, it sets only the selection color.

underlineColor.description: Inactive underline color of the input.

activeUnderlineColor.description: Active underline color of the input.

outlineColor.description: Inactive outline color of the input.

activeOutlineColor.description: Active outline color of the input.

textColor.description: Color of the text in the input.

dense.description:
Sets min height with densed layout. For `TextInput` in `flat` mode
height is `64dp` or in dense layout - `52dp` with label or `40dp` without label.
For `TextInput` in `outlined` mode
height is `56dp` or in dense layout - `40dp` regardless of label.
When you apply `height` prop in style the `dense` prop affects only `paddingVertical` inside `TextInput`

multiline.description: Whether the input can have multiple lines.

numberOfLines.description:
@platform Android only
The number of lines to show in the input (Android only).

onFocus.description: Callback that is called when the text input is focused.

onBlur.description: Callback that is called when the text input is blurred.

render.description:
Callback to render a custom input component such as `react-native-text-input-mask`
instead of the default `TextInput` component from `react-native`.

Example:
```js
<TextInput
  label="Phone number"
  render={props =>
    <TextInputMask
      {...props}
      mask="+[00] [000] [000] [000]"
    />
  }
/>
```

style.description:
Pass `fontSize` prop to modify the font size inside `TextInput`.
Pass `height` prop to set `TextInput` height. When `height` is passed,
`dense` prop will affect only input's `paddingVertical`.
Pass `paddingHorizontal` to modify horizontal padding.
This can be used to get MD Guidelines v1 TextInput look.

testID.description: testID to be used on tests.

contentStyle.description:
Pass custom style directly to the input itself.
Overrides input style
Example: `paddingLeft`, `backgroundColor`

outlineStyle.description:
Pass style to override the default style of outlined wrapper.
Overrides style when mode is set to `outlined`
Example: `borderRadius`, `borderColor`

underlineStyle.description:
Pass style to override the default style of underlined wrapper.
Overrides style when mode is set to `flat`
Example: `borderRadius`, `borderColor`

TextInput/Adornment/TextInputAffix.title: TextInput.Affix

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the affix. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the affix.

textStyle.description: Style that is passed to the Text element.

TextInput/Adornment/TextInputIcon.title: TextInput.Icon

icon.description:
@renamed Renamed from 'name' to 'icon` in v5.x
Icon to show.

forceTextInputFocus.description: Whether the TextInput will focus after onPress.

color.description: Color of the icon or a function receiving a boolean indicating whether the TextInput is focused and returning the color.

ToggleButton/ToggleButton.title: ToggleButton

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled.

accessibilityLabel.description: Accessibility label for the `ToggleButton`. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the button.

testID.description: testID to be used on tests.

ToggleButton/ToggleButtonGroup.title: ToggleButton.Group

onValueChange.description: Function to execute on selection change.

value.description: Value of the currently selected toggle button.

ToggleButton/ToggleButtonRow.title: ToggleButton.Row

onValueChange.description: Function to execute on selection change.

value.description: Value of the currently selected toggle button.

Tooltip/Tooltip.title: Tooltip

children.description: Tooltip reference element. Needs to be able to hold a ref.

enterTouchDelay.description: The number of milliseconds a user must touch the element before showing the tooltip.

leaveTouchDelay.description: The number of milliseconds after the user stops touching an element before hiding the tooltip.

titleMaxFontSizeMultiplier.description: Specifies the largest possible scale a title font can reach.

TouchableRipple/TouchableRipple.title: TouchableRipple

borderless.description: Whether to render the ripple outside the view bounds.

background.description:
Type of background drawabale to display the feedback (Android).
https://reactnative.dev/docs/pressable#rippleconfig

centered.description: Whether to start the ripple at the center (Web).

disabled.description: Whether to prevent interaction with the touchable.

onPress.description: Function to execute on press. If not set, will cause the touchable to be disabled.

onLongPress.description: Function to execute on long press.

onPressIn.description: Function to execute immediately when a touch is engaged, before `onPressOut` and `onPress`.

onPressOut.description: Function to execute when a touch is released.

rippleColor.description: Color of the ripple effect (Android >= 5.0 and Web).

underlayColor.description: Color of the underlay for the highlight effect (Android < 5.0 and iOS).

Typography/Text.title: Text
Typography/Text.description:
Typography component showing styles complied with passed `variant` prop and supported by the type system.

## Usage
```js
import * as React from 'react';
import { Text } from 'react-native-paper';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <>
    <Text variant="displayLarge">Display Large</Text>
    <Text variant="displayMedium">Display Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="displaySmall">Display small</Text>

    <Text variant="headlineLarge">Headline Large</Text>
    <Text variant="headlineMedium">Headline Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="headlineSmall">Headline Small</Text>

    <Text variant="titleLarge">Title Large</Text>
    <Text variant="titleMedium">Title Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="titleSmall">Title Small</Text>

    <Text variant="bodyLarge">Body Large</Text>
    <Text variant="bodyMedium">Body Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="bodySmall">Body Small</Text>

    <Text variant="labelLarge">Label Large</Text>
    <Text variant="labelMedium">Label Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="labelSmall">Label Small</Text>
 </>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

@extends Text props https://reactnative.dev/docs/text#props

data.description:
Typography component showing styles complied with passed `variant` prop and supported by the type system.

## Usage
```js
import * as React from 'react';
import { Text } from 'react-native-paper';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <>
    <Text variant="displayLarge">Display Large</Text>
    <Text variant="displayMedium">Display Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="displaySmall">Display small</Text>

    <Text variant="headlineLarge">Headline Large</Text>
    <Text variant="headlineMedium">Headline Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="headlineSmall">Headline Small</Text>

    <Text variant="titleLarge">Title Large</Text>
    <Text variant="titleMedium">Title Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="titleSmall">Title Small</Text>

    <Text variant="bodyLarge">Body Large</Text>
    <Text variant="bodyMedium">Body Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="bodySmall">Body Small</Text>

    <Text variant="labelLarge">Label Large</Text>
    <Text variant="labelMedium">Label Medium</Text>
    <Text variant="labelSmall">Label Small</Text>
 </>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

@extends Text props https://reactnative.dev/docs/text#props

variant.description:
@supported Available in v5.x with theme version 3

Variant defines appropriate text styles for type role and its size.
Available variants:

 Display: `displayLarge`, `displayMedium`, `displaySmall`

 Headline: `headlineLarge`, `headlineMedium`, `headlineSmall`

 Title: `titleLarge`, `titleMedium`, `titleSmall`

 Label:  `labelLarge`, `labelMedium`, `labelSmall`

 Body: `bodyLarge`, `bodyMedium`, `bodySmall`
